title: calophyllum calaba var. bracteatum
intro: Calophyllum calaba var. bracteatum, also known as the Philippine oak or Acacia tree, is a rare species of plant in the family Calophyllaceae. It is known for its large, leathery leaves and its ability to be used in traditional medicine. Calophyllum calaba var. bracteatum has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine for its health benefits and medicinal uses.
